title: The Prospective Sense of Agency is Rooted in Local and Global Properties of Intrinsic Functional Brain Networks.

assets.abstract: The sense of agency (SoA) refers to a constitutional aspect of the self describing the extent to which individuals feel in control over their actions and consequences thereof. Although the SoA has been associated with mental health and well-being, it is still unknown how interindividual variability in the SoA is embedded in the intrinsic brain organization. We hypothesized that the prospective component of an implicit SoA is associated with brain networks related to SoA and sensorimotor predicti

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Nested agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.
